arts.sum: Celtic win and Celtic reach the Champions League knockout stages – simple as that. Follow it here LIVE

arts.ttl: Champions League preview: Celtic v Young Boys
arts.sum: We preview Celtic’s crunch Champions League match with Young Boys Bern.

arts.ttl: Arsenal v Dinamo Zagreb, Real Madrid v Salzburg, Celtic v Young Boys and more: Champions League – live
arts.sum: Updates from Wednesday’s big European games Live scoreboard | Sign up for Football Daily | Email Simon Ahead of tonight’s game Mikel Arteta has been talking about the power of music, and Ed Aarons has been hearing him do it: Music is very much part of our culture. We have music in a lot of different places, and I think music has the capacity to change your mood, to change your energy to a certain place. And I think it happens in all of us. You play a song and immediately you feel different, and we have certain songs that trigger something in our team because they have some history as well with us, and I will use it when we believe it’s the right way to do it. I have a lot, depending on the day, that’s what I’m saying. It’s not about my energy, and sometimes I play a song many, many times. I don’t know, it’s a feeling. That is something that we’ve done for many years now and it is part of training. We use music as another element of our training sessions to build the energy and change certain purposes that we want in the training session, and I think the players enjoy it. Depending on the day, sometimes certain players pick certain songs - the first song, the first two songs, the last song - it’s the same as in the dressing room really. Continue reading...

arts.sum: The striker is a man in-demand as the January window enters its closing stages

arts.ttl: Arne Engels can't believe what he has witnessed with Celtic in the Champions League at Parkhead
arts.sum: The last time Celtic played in Europe was on December 10, and since then, this Young Boys match and the enormity of it has been building up. After a small break, Champions League action returns and Celtic welcome Young Boys to Parkhead knowing that destiny is in their hands if they want to progress to the play-off phase.

arts.ttl: Kyle Vassell states who is more difficult to face - Celtic's Auston Trusty or Liam Scales
arts.sum: The 31-year-old told Open Goal that Scales is ‘a little bit smarter’ and he likes the challenge of Trusty because he prefers being more physical. It wasn’t a great game for the American, but his fellow centre-back didn’t exactly showcase anything better during that Ibrox horror show after the New Year, so what is it like facing the duo?

arts.ttl: Ally McCoist called 'salty' as Alan Brazil and Shebahn Aherne defend Celtic in Champions League
arts.sum: And Celtic-mad Alan Brazil feels it makes that trip down south ‘harder’ because Villa won’t rest players now knowing that they need a win, as he told talkSPORT (22/01/25 at 7:50 am). Aherne labelled McCoist ‘salty’ because Celtic are in Europe’s elite competition, whilst Brazil reminded his fellow countryman of what trophy sits in the Parkhead cabinet room that Rangers will never win.

arts.ttl: Celtic need to hurry as rivals ready to ‘meet the price’ and seal signing
arts.sum: There’s a claim in the Turkish media that Fenerbahce are considering an offer for Hammarby attacker Bazoumana Touré, who has also been linked with Celtic in recent days. Both Jose Mourinho and Brendan Rodgers are looking to add to their squads during this transfer window, and the 18-year-old has emerged as a target for both The post Celtic need to hurry as rivals ready to ‘meet the price’ and seal signing appeared first on Sport Witness.
